source_jobs.title: Head of Growth
source_jobs.description:
VidRush is an AI-native video production platform that replaces an entire video team with coordinated AI agents.



Creators turn a simple idea into a fully produced, long-form video—research, script, voiceover, visuals, motion graphics, and rendering—in under an hour. No timelines. No editing headaches. Just intent → finished video.



We’re building a new way to create video that feels more like writing than editing, and we’re already seeing strong traction from serious creators and media teams.



If you’re excited about AI, creator tools, and building category-defining products from first principles, you’ll fit right in.

VidRush is building toward Artificial General Editing Intelligence (AGEI) — AI that can autonomously edit long-form YouTube videos at the level of a skilled human editor.
We started in faceless YouTube content and are expanding into YouTube talking-head creators, founders, coaches, agencies, and personal brands.

Our promise:
Record → Upload A-roll/B-roll → Get a $100+ quality edit in minutes → Refine via chat (Rush Agent).


We’re planning a major public launch and aggressive scale phase.



The Role
We’re hiring a Head of Growth to architect and execute VidRush’s growth engine end-to-end.
This is not a “marketing manager” role.
This is a revenue and distribution ownership role.
You will operate like the GM of growth: strategy + execution + hiring.


What You’ll Own
Growth Strategy + Unit Economics

 - Own growth targets across acquisition channels

 - Build a plan to hit and maintain 4x+ ROAS targets where applicable

 - Manage blended CAC goals and budget allocation

 - Define ICP priorities (talking head creators, businesses using YouTube as a funnel, etc.)

 - Build reporting cadence and dashboards (CAC, ROAS, payback, cohort retention)

Creator Partnerships + YouTube Sponsorships

 - Identify, negotiate, and manage YouTube sponsorship deals

 - Create repeatable sponsorship playbooks and scripts

 - Build a pipeline of creators and placements that convert

 - Track conversion per creator and double down on winners

Affiliate Program (Creator-Led)

 - Build and scale an affiliate engine with creator-friendly onboarding

 - Define commission structures and tiers (profit-aware)

 - Recruit and support top affiliates with assets and tracking

 - Monitor cohort quality and retention by affiliate source

UGC Engine + Viral Distribution

 - Recruit/manage UGC creators and build a repeatable creative pipeline

 - Run high-velocity creative testing (hooks, angles, formats)

 - Identify viral outliers and turn them into repeatable templates

 - Coordinate with a performance marketer (or hire one) to scale winning creatives into paid

Team + Execution

 - Hire and manage specialists as needed (UGC ops, partnerships, paid, creative)

 - Build a lean, high-output growth org

 - Move fast, iterate, and ship

What We’re Looking For
You’ve done this before. You have receipts.
Required:

 - Proven experience scaling a creator tool, consumer SaaS, mobile subscription app, or high-growth B2C product

 - Strong understanding of creator distribution (sponsorships, affiliates, UGC)

 - Ability to run growth as a system: experiments, metrics, accountability

 - Comfortable hiring and managing specialists

 - Startup/operator mindset: scrappy, fast, high ownership

Nice to have:

 - Experience with YouTube creator ecosystems

 - Experience scaling from early traction to meaningful ARR

 - Experience operating with tight margin constraints and CAC discipline

Working Style

 - High autonomy

 - Direct communication

 - Fast iteration cycles

 - Ownership mindset

Compensation & Benefits

 - Competitive full-time salary (no equity)

 - Fully remote

 - Potential bi-yearly hacker houses / team retreats

 - High ownership + direct impact on a category-defining product
   

Full-Time • Remote • Competitive Salary • Team Retreats (2x/year)

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer (TypeScript/React + AWS Cloud) - AI / Agentic Applications
source_jobs.description:
VidRush is an AI-native video production platform that replaces an entire video team with coordinated AI agents.



Creators turn a simple idea into a fully produced, long-form video—research, script, voiceover, visuals, motion graphics, and rendering—in under an hour. No timelines. No editing headaches. Just intent → finished video.



We’re building a new way to create video that feels more like writing than editing, and we’re already seeing strong traction from serious creators and media teams.



If you’re excited about AI, creator tools, and building category-defining products from first principles, you’ll fit right in.

We're looking for a backend engineer to help build and scale the infrastructure behind Vidrush, a video generation platform. You'll work on a TypeScript/Node.js serverless backend where code quality and thoughtful design are first-class priorities.

What you'll do:

 - Own and deliver backend features end-to-end, from API design through deployment

 - Solve interesting problems across video generation pipelines, AI integrations, and billing systems

 - Shape the architecture of services that power our core product workflows

 - Contribute to a codebase that prioritizes long-term maintainability

 - Collaborate closely with the team on technical decisions, code reviews, and continuous improvement
   
   

Requirements

- 2–7 years of professional backend engineering experience

- Strong proficiency in TypeScript and Node.js

- Hands-on experience with AWS in a serverless context

- Solid experience with MongoDB or other document databases

- Experience with Stripe or similar payment/billing platforms

- Familiarity with serverless frameworks (e.g., SST, Serverless Framework)

- Experience writing unit and integration tests with Jest or similar

- Understanding of RESTful API design and middleware patterns

- Comfortable with Git workflows, CI/CD, and code quality tooling

- Experience in building AI-powered applications, especially agentic systems using LLMs in production.



Nice to Have

- Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Pulumi, CDK, Terraform)

- Familiarity with event-driven architectures and messaging patterns

- Background in layered architecture patterns (Clean Architecture, hexagonal, ports & adapters)

- Experience with modern web frameworks like Hono

- Interest in AI/LLM technologies and experience integrating with AI APIs

- Familiarity with agentic AI systems and frameworks like Mastra



What We Value

 - Craftsmanship: You care about code quality, testing, and maintainability—not just making it work

 - Architectural discipline: You respect layer boundaries and established patterns rather than taking shortcuts

 - Pragmatism: You ship working software without over-engineering

 - Ownership: You take features end-to-end, from design through production

 - AI-accelerated development: You actively use tools like Cursor, Copilot, and LLMs for scaffolding, tests, refactors, and debugging—without outsourcing your judgment

 - Curiosity about AI: You're excited about the intersection of backend engineering and AI capabilities

Note: whilst we're a global company working remotely, we prefer to hire from Turkey - as most of our Engineering team is localized there.

source_jobs.title: Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
VidRush is an AI-native video production platform that replaces an entire video team with coordinated AI agents.



Creators turn a simple idea into a fully produced, long-form video—research, script, voiceover, visuals, motion graphics, and rendering—in under an hour. No timelines. No editing headaches. Just intent → finished video.



We’re building a new way to create video that feels more like writing than editing, and we’re already seeing strong traction from serious creators and media teams.



If you’re excited about AI, creator tools, and building category-defining products from first principles, you’ll fit right in.

We're looking for a Marketing Manager based in Dubai to own growth across every channel that matters. You'll be a growth hacker at heart—someone who lives and breathes social media, knows how each platform actually works, and turns that knowledge into real business results. You don't need to be a world-class expert in all of them, but you do need range: the instinct to test, learn, and double down on what drives traction.


What you'll do:


Own and execute growth strategy end-to-end, from experiment design through measurable results

Build and run campaigns across social platforms—YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, X, Threads, and whatever comes next

Identify the highest-leverage channels for reaching creators and media teams, then go deep

Create and adapt content that fits the native voice of each platform

Set up tracking, analyze performance, and let data guide where you invest next

Partner with product and content teams to turn our story into demand

Spot emerging platforms and trends early, and move fast to capitalize on them


Requirements


3–7 years of marketing experience, ideally with a growth or performance focus

Hands-on, proven experience growing audiences and driving conversion across multiple social platforms

Versatility: comfortable jumping between platforms and adapting strategy to each one's mechanics

Strong grasp of organic and paid social, plus the interplay between them

Data fluency—you can read analytics, run A/B tests, and make decisions from numbers

Excellent content instincts and an eye for what makes people stop scrolling

Fluent in English (our team and creators are global)

A self-starter mindset: you find opportunities and act without waiting for a playbook


Nice to Have


Experience marketing to creators, video professionals, or media teams

Familiarity with the SaaS or creator-tools landscape

Hands-on experience with AI tools for content creation, ideation, or campaign workflows

Community building or influencer/partnership marketing experience

Experience scaling a product in its early stages

Comfort with marketing automation, analytics, and attribution tools


What We Value


Versatility: You're not boxed into one channel or tactic—you adapt to where the growth is

Experimentation: You treat marketing like a series of tests, learning fast and iterating faster

Ownership: You take campaigns end-to-end, from idea through results

Data-driven instinct: You back your creative bets with numbers and know when to kill what isn't working

Native platform fluency: You understand what makes each social platform tick and speak its language

AI-accelerated marketing: You actively use AI tools to move faster on content, research, and campaigns—without outsourcing your judgment

Curiosity about AI: You're excited about the intersection of marketing and AI-native products

source_jobs.title: Software Sales Agent (Onboarding + Demos)
source_jobs.description:
VidRush is an AI-native video production platform that replaces an entire video team with coordinated AI agents.



Creators turn a simple idea into a fully produced, long-form video—research, script, voiceover, visuals, motion graphics, and rendering—in under an hour. No timelines. No editing headaches. Just intent → finished video.



We’re building a new way to create video that feels more like writing than editing, and we’re already seeing strong traction from serious creators and media teams.



If you’re excited about AI, creator tools, and building category-defining products from first principles, you’ll fit right in.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Software Sales Agent (Salaried) who will be the first human touchpoint for many of our customers.

Your job is to:

 1. run onboarding / walkthrough calls

 2. explain the product clearly to new users

 3. help users choose the right plan

 4. answer sales questions (features, use cases, pricing, workflows)

 5. be available for on-demand scheduled calls when users request help

This is a role for someone who is naturally helpful, clear, and organized — someone who can sell through education.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 1. Run onboarding calls for new customers and help them get set up quickly

 2. Host product demos and answer pre-sales questions

 3. Help users understand how to get the best results with the tool

 4. Handle inbound leads who request a call (scheduled on demand)

 5. Guide users to the right plan based on their goals and usage

 6. Follow up with users who are stuck or haven’t activated successfully

 7. Document common issues/questions and share them with the team to improve onboarding and product

 8. Track call outcomes and feedback so we continuously improve conversion and retention




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR




MOST IMPORTANT

 1. Strong spoken English and excellent communication skills

 2. Confident running calls with customers (friendly, structured, calm)

 3. Ability to explain software clearly to non-technical users

 4. Comfortable answering sales questions without being pushy

 5. High responsiveness: you reply quickly and follow through

 6. Organized: you keep notes, manage scheduling, and stay on top of follow-ups

 7. Ownership mindset: you solve problems instead of passing them around




GREAT FIT IF YOU’VE BEEN:

 1. a SaaS onboarding specialist / customer success rep

 2. a sales development rep who prefers demos over cold outreach

 3. a support person who’s naturally good at closing through education

 4. a customer-facing operator in a startup environment




NICE TO HAVE

 1. Experience in SaaS sales, onboarding, customer success, or technical support

 2. Experience with CRM tools (HubSpot, Salesforce, etc.)

 3. Familiarity with creator tools / YouTube workflows

 4. Comfortable recording short Loom-style walkthrough videos

 5. Experience handling pricing, billing, or plan upgrade questions




TOOLS YOU’LL USE

 1. Scheduling system (Calendly or similar)

 2. CRM (or lightweight tracking)

 3. Slack / Discord

 4. Email + chat support systems




HOW WE WORK

 1. Small team, fast feedback loops

 2. You’ll work closely with founders and the product team

 3. Your insights from calls will directly shape the product and onboarding

source_jobs.title: Automation QA Engineer
source_jobs.description:
VidRush is an AI-native video production platform that replaces an entire video team with coordinated AI agents.



Creators turn a simple idea into a fully produced, long-form video—research, script, voiceover, visuals, motion graphics, and rendering—in under an hour. No timelines. No editing headaches. Just intent → finished video.



We’re building a new way to create video that feels more like writing than editing, and we’re already seeing strong traction from serious creators and media teams.



If you’re excited about AI, creator tools, and building category-defining products from first principles, you’ll fit right in.

We're looking for an Automation QA Engineer (AQA) based in Turkey to own quality across Vidrush, an AI-native video generation platform. You'll build and maintain the automated testing infrastructure that keeps a fast-moving product reliable—designing test frameworks, catching regressions before they ship, and making sure complex video generation pipelines behave exactly as intended. You'll work closely with engineering in a TypeScript/Node.js environment where quality is a first-class priority, not an afterthought.



What you'll do:



Design, build, and maintain automated test suites across the platform—API, integration, and end-to-end

Own test coverage for critical workflows: video generation pipelines, AI integrations, and billing

Build and improve testing frameworks and tooling that scale with the product

Integrate automated tests into CI/CD so quality checks run on every change

Investigate failures, identify root causes, and partner with engineers on fixes

Define test strategy for new features, from edge cases to performance and reliability

Help shape a culture where quality is shared ownership across the team



Requirements



2–7 years of professional QA experience with a strong automation focus

Solid proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript (our stack is Node.js-based)

Hands-on experience with modern test automation frameworks (e.g., Playwright, Cypress, Jest)

Strong experience with API testing (REST) and tools like Postman or similar

Experience building and maintaining end-to-end and integration test suites

Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and integrating automated tests into them

Understanding of testing strategy: when to unit, integrate, or e2e

Comfortable with Git workflows and collaborating in a code-review culture

Strong analytical skills and attention to detail



Nice to Have

Experience testing AI/LLM-powered features or non-deterministic systems

Familiarity with AWS or serverless architectures

Experience with performance and load testing tools

Exposure to MongoDB or other document databases

Experience testing payment/billing flows (e.g., Stripe)

Familiarity with event-driven architectures and asynchronous workflows

Experience with visual or media-based testing



What We Value

Craftsmanship: You care about reliable, maintainable tests—not just passing checks

Ownership: You take quality end-to-end, from test design through CI integration

Pragmatism: You know what's worth automating and what isn't—no testing for testing's sake

Curiosity: You dig into root causes rather than papering over flaky tests

AI-accelerated work: You actively use tools like Cursor, Copilot, and LLMs to write tests, debug, and move faster—without outsourcing your judgment

Excitement about AI: You're energized by the challenge of testing AI-native products where outputs aren't always deterministic

source_jobs.title: Senior Python Backend Engineer (AI / Agents)
source_jobs.description:
VidRush is an AI-native video production platform that replaces an entire video team with coordinated AI agents.



Creators turn a simple idea into a fully produced, long-form video—research, script, voiceover, visuals, motion graphics, and rendering—in under an hour. No timelines. No editing headaches. Just intent → finished video.



We’re building a new way to create video that feels more like writing than editing, and we’re already seeing strong traction from serious creators and media teams.



If you’re excited about AI, creator tools, and building category-defining products from first principles, you’ll fit right in.

We’re hiring a Senior Python Backend Engineer (AI Agents) to help build and scale Vidrush’s AI-driven media pipeline. Vidrush is building an agentic pipeline that can turn a prompt into a full video production workflow — including script generation, voiceover, footage selection, and assembly — and we are scaling the backend team quickly to accelerate execution.



This role is fundamentally about shipping and ownership. We’re not looking for a “perfect on paper” engineer who optimizes for theoretical correctness or academic knowledge. We want someone who can take ambiguous problems, move fast, and reliably deliver tangible progress without needing to be managed.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build agentic systems that can reason, plan, and execute multi-step tasks reliably

 - Design and deploy LLM-based services (tool use, structured outputs, evals, monitoring)

 - Develop video/image intelligence pipelines (retrieval, matching, classification, metadata extraction, generative augmentation)

 - Integrate existing APIs and tools when appropriate (strong build-vs-buy judgment)

 - Ship scalable inference pipelines on cloud infrastructure

 - Apply MLOps / LLMOps best practices: observability, testing, evals, versioning, cost control

 - Work closely with founders and product to iterate quickly based on user feedback
   
   


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong Python engineering skills and proven ability to ship production systems

 - Experience building with LLMs (OpenAI-style APIs, tool calling, RAG, structured generation)

 - Experience deploying ML/LLM services in production (cloud, containers, serverless, async pipelines)

 - Practical understanding of video/image processing (CV or media pipelines)

 - High autonomy: you can take vague problems and turn them into shipped solutions

 - Strong engineering fundamentals: clean code, reliability, performance, and cost awareness




NICE TO HAVE

 - Agent orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, etc.)

 - Experience with vector search / embeddings / retrieval systems

 - Experience with workflow orchestration (queues, batch jobs, event-driven systems)

 - Experience with PyTorch and GPU inference optimization

 - Experience working on media-heavy products (video editing, video generation, large file pipelines)
   
   


HOW WE WORK

 - Small team, high trust, high ownership

 - Bias to action and shipping

 - Lightweight specs, fast iteration

 - Strong focus on developer experience, testing where it matters, and observability



Location preference

 - Turkey

source_jobs.title: Product Manager (Product + Execution)
source_jobs.description:
VidRush is an AI-native video production platform that replaces an entire video team with coordinated AI agents.



Creators turn a simple idea into a fully produced, long-form video—research, script, voiceover, visuals, motion graphics, and rendering—in under an hour. No timelines. No editing headaches. Just intent → finished video.



We’re building a new way to create video that feels more like writing than editing, and we’re already seeing strong traction from serious creators and media teams.



If you’re excited about AI, creator tools, and building category-defining products from first principles, you’ll fit right in.






THE ROLE


We’re hiring a Product Manager to streamline execution and product delivery across multiple teams, including both standard development and R&D/AI work.

You’ll work directly with founders and engineering leads to:

 1. define strategy and priorities

 2. translate that strategy into clear requirements

 3. manage cross-team execution in Jira

 4. enforce accountability, visibility, and predictable delivery

 5. help the team ship high-quality work faster without chaos

This role blends classic product thinking with strong Product Ops / Delivery instincts.



WHAT YOU’LL DO



STRATEGY & PRIORITIZATION

 1. Help define the product strategy for the next 4–12 weeks

 2. Translate company goals into a focused roadmap

 3. Make tradeoffs and say “no” when necessary to protect velocity



REQUIREMENTS & PLANNING

 1. Write clear, concrete feature requirements before development starts

 2. Align frontend and backend expectations early to avoid mismatches late

 3. Define ownership: who’s responsible for what, and what “done” means




EXECUTION & ACCOUNTABILITY

 1. Own Jira: maintain a clean backlog, run sprint planning, and drive follow-through

 2. Track progress and surface blockers early (before a task stalls for weeks)

 3. Improve team velocity and visibility across time zones




MANAGING R&D / AI WORK

 1. Structure exploration work using “spikes” and experiments

 2. Reduce endless trial-and-error by defining:

 3. why the experiment exists

 4. what success looks like

 5. how long it runs before a decision is made

 6. Keep research aligned to strategy rather than constantly chasing new trends




TEAM PROCESS

 1. Establish lightweight rituals that actually help (standups, planning, retros)

 2. Create meeting structures that work across time zones (e.g., two-pass sync + notes)

 3. Improve internal documentation and communication flow




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR (MOST IMPORTANT)

 1. Strong experience shipping product in fast-moving environments

 2. Excellent at writing requirements that eliminate ambiguity

 3. Confident prioritizer: can protect focus and reduce roadmap chaos

 4. Highly organized and execution-driven: you push work to completion

 5. Deep Jira experience (backlog hygiene, sprint planning, workflows)

 6. Able to coordinate distributed teams across time zones

 7. Great communicator: you align stakeholders without creating friction

 8. Comfortable working with technical teams, including AI/R&D workflows




NICE TO HAVE

 1. Experience with creator tools, media products, or video workflows

 2. Experience managing hybrid teams (product + R&D + engineering)

 3. Strong background as a business analyst or product ops lead

 4. Experience building internal systems/processes from scratch




HOW WE WORK

 1. Small team, high ownership — you’ll drive outcomes directly

 2. Lightweight process: crisp requirements, fast iteration, measurable results

 3. Strong bias to action and clarity

 4. We value execution, not performative process

source_jobs.title: Head of Product / Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
VidRush is an AI-native video production platform that replaces an entire video team with coordinated AI agents.



Creators turn a simple idea into a fully produced, long-form video—research, script, voiceover, visuals, motion graphics, and rendering—in under an hour. No timelines. No editing headaches. Just intent → finished video.



We’re building a new way to create video that feels more like writing than editing, and we’re already seeing strong traction from serious creators and media teams.



If you’re excited about AI, creator tools, and building category-defining products from first principles, you’ll fit right in.

Department: Product

Reports To: CEO

Location: Remote (Turkey strongly preferred / UTC+3)




WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

We have a talented engineering team and junior PM support, but we lack a strategic anchor. Currently, the CEO is the only bridge between business goals and technical execution, leading to vague PRDs and sprints cluttered with low-priority tasks.

We need a Head of Product with 3–7 years of experience to take full ownership of the "What" and "Why." You will be the person who translates high-level vision into airtight requirements, holds the team accountable to high quality standards, and relentlessly cuts anything that doesn't drive revenue or retention.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Strategy & Roadmap Ownership

 - Define the 4–12 week product strategy with clear milestones and success criteria.

 - Act as the final filter for the roadmap: evaluate every feature against revenue impact and growth potential.

 - Conduct deep competitor analysis to ensure VidRush maintains its "category-defining" edge.



High-Fidelity Execution

 - Airtight PRDs: Take over and refine all Product Requirement Documents. Every spec must include acceptance criteria, edge cases, and a clear split between frontend/backend responsibilities to eliminate developer guesswork.

 - Jira Mastery: Own the backlog hygiene and sprint planning. Move beyond "moving tickets" to using velocity data to improve delivery predictability.

 - Process Design: Establish lightweight rituals (two-pass syncs, async notes) that work across distributed time zones.

Managing R&D & AI Work

 - Structure unpredictable AI experimentation using time-boxed "spikes."

 - Bridge the gap between R&D exploration and product reality, ensuring research stays aligned with business goals rather than chasing every new model trend.

Team Leadership

 - Manage and mentor the existing PM team.

 - Be the "First Line of Defense" against scope creep, pushing back on bad ideas—even from the CEO—to protect team velocity.

What We’re Looking For

 - Experience: 3–7 years of Product Management experience, preferably at a high-growth startup (YC-backed, Series A+, or top-tier Turkish tech).

 - AI Literacy: You must understand the nuances of building with non-deterministic systems (LLMs/Generative AI) and how that impacts roadmap planning.

 - Technical Writing: You take pride in documentation. Your requirements leave zero room for ambiguity.

 - Execution-Driven: You have a "Bias to Action." You prefer a shipped feature over a perfect slide deck.

 - Customer Obsession: You are comfortable jumping into Discord or hopping on calls with power users to turn messy feedback into actionable insights.

 - Communication: Direct, candid, and persuasive. You align stakeholders without creating friction.




HOW WE WORK

 - Small Team, High Ownership: You will drive outcomes directly. There is no room for performative process here.

 - Speed is a Feature: We value crisp requirements followed by fast iteration.

 - Remote-First: We coordinate across time zones but require a minimum of 6 hours overlap with Turkish business hours (UTC+3).

Ready to define the future of AI video? > We value execution over everything else. If you have a track record of shipping high-impact products and want to lead a category-defining platform, let’s talk.

Ownership: You take campaigns end-to-end, from idea through results

Data-driven instinct: You back your creative bets with numbers and know when to kill what isn't working

Native platform fluency: You understand what makes each social platform tick and speak its language

AI-accelerated marketing: You actively use AI tools to move faster on content, research, and campaigns—without outsourcing your judgment

Curiosity about AI: You're excited about the intersection of marketing and AI-native products

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer (TypeScript/React + AWS Cloud) - Stripe / Billing systems
source_jobs.description:
VidRush is an AI-native video production platform that replaces an entire video team with coordinated AI agents.



Creators turn a simple idea into a fully produced, long-form video—research, script, voiceover, visuals, motion graphics, and rendering—in under an hour. No timelines. No editing headaches. Just intent → finished video.



We’re building a new way to create video that feels more like writing than editing, and we’re already seeing strong traction from serious creators and media teams.



If you’re excited about AI, creator tools, and building category-defining products from first principles, you’ll fit right in.

We're looking for a backend engineer to help build and scale the infrastructure behind Vidrush, a video generation platform. You'll work on a TypeScript/Node.js serverless backend where code quality and thoughtful design are first-class priorities.

What you'll do:

 - Own and deliver backend features end-to-end, from API design through deployment

 - Solve interesting problems across video generation pipelines, AI integrations, and billing systems

 - Shape the architecture of services that power our core product workflows

 - Contribute to a codebase that prioritizes long-term maintainability

 - Collaborate closely with the team on technical decisions, code reviews, and continuous improvement
   
   

Requirements

- 2–7 years of professional backend engineering experience

- Strong proficiency in TypeScript and Node.js

- Hands-on experience with AWS in a serverless context

- Solid experience with MongoDB or other document databases

- Experience with Stripe or similar payment/billing platforms

- Familiarity with serverless frameworks (e.g., SST, Serverless Framework)

- Experience writing unit and integration tests with Jest or similar

- Understanding of RESTful API design and middleware patterns

- Comfortable with Git workflows, CI/CD, and code quality tooling

- Specialization in Stripe, complex product structures, subscriptions, usage-based billing, invoicing, payment workflows, and production-scale billing operations.



Nice to Have

- Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Pulumi, CDK, Terraform)

- Familiarity with event-driven architectures and messaging patterns

- Background in layered architecture patterns (Clean Architecture, hexagonal, ports & adapters)

- Experience with modern web frameworks like Hono

- Interest in AI/LLM technologies and experience integrating with AI APIs

- Familiarity with agentic AI systems and frameworks like Mastra



What We Value

 - Craftsmanship: You care about code quality, testing, and maintainability—not just making it work

 - Architectural discipline: You respect layer boundaries and established patterns rather than taking shortcuts

 - Pragmatism: You ship working software without over-engineering

 - Ownership: You take features end-to-end, from design through production

 - AI-accelerated development: You actively use tools like Cursor, Copilot, and LLMs for scaffolding, tests, refactors, and debugging—without outsourcing your judgment

 - Curiosity about AI: You're excited about the intersection of backend engineering and AI capabilities

Note: whilst we're a global company working remotely, we prefer to hire from Turkey - as most of our Engineering team is localized there.

notes: AI-native video production platform with strong engineering focus
